body:
Depends on: none

## Goal

The disinto website becomes a versioned artifact: built by CI, published to Codeberg's generic package registry, deployed to staging automatically. Version visible in footer.

## Files to add/change

### `site/VERSION`
```
0.1.0
```

### `site/build.sh`
```bash
#!/bin/bash
VERSION=$(cat VERSION)
mkdir -p dist
cp *.html *.jpg *.webp *.png *.ico *.xml robots.txt dist/
sed -i "s|Built from scrap, powered by a single battery.|v${VERSION} · Built from scrap, powered by a single battery.|" dist/index.html
echo "$VERSION" > dist/VERSION
```

### `site/index.html`
No template placeholder needed — `build.sh` does the sed replacement on the existing footer text.

### `.woodpecker/site.yml`
```yaml
when:
  path: "site/**"
  event: push
  branch: main

steps:
  - name: build
    image: alpine
    commands:
      - cd site && sh build.sh
      - VERSION=$(cat site/VERSION)
      - tar czf site-${VERSION}.tar.gz -C site/dist .

  - name: publish
    image: alpine
    commands:
      - apk add curl
      - VERSION=$(cat site/VERSION)
      - >-
        curl -sf --user "johba:$$FORGE_TOKEN"
        --upload-file site-${VERSION}.tar.gz
        "https://codeberg.org/api/packages/johba/generic/disinto-site/${VERSION}/site-${VERSION}.tar.gz"
    environment:
      FORGE_TOKEN:
        from_secret: forge_token

  - name: deploy-staging
    image: alpine
    commands:
      - apk add curl
      - VERSION=$(cat site/VERSION)
      - >-
        curl -sf --user "johba:$$FORGE_TOKEN"
        "https://codeberg.org/api/packages/johba/generic/disinto-site/${VERSION}/site-${VERSION}.tar.gz"
        -o site.tar.gz
      - rm -rf /srv/staging/*
      - tar xzf site.tar.gz -C /srv/staging/
    environment:
      FORGE_TOKEN:
        from_secret: forge_token
    volumes:
      - /home/debian/staging-site:/srv/staging
```

## Infra setup (manual, before first run)
- `mkdir -p /home/debian/staging-site`
- Add to Caddyfile: `staging.disinto.ai { root * /home/debian/staging-site; file_server }`
- DNS: `staging.disinto.ai` A record → same IP as `disinto.ai`
- Reload Caddy: `sudo systemctl reload caddy`
- Add `forge_token` as Woodpecker repo secret for johba/disinto (if not already set)
- Add `/home/debian/staging-site` to `WOODPECKER_BACKEND_DOCKER_VOLUMES`

## Verification
- [ ] Merge PR that touches `site/` → CI runs site pipeline
- [ ] Package appears at `codeberg.org/johba/-/packages/generic/disinto-site/0.1.0`
- [ ] `staging.disinto.ai` serves the site with `v0.1.0` in footer
- [ ] `disinto.ai` (production) unchanged

## Related
- #764 — docker stack edge proxy + staging (future: this moves inside the stack)
- #755 — vault-gated production promotion (production deploy comes later)

## Affected files
- `site/VERSION` — new, holds current version string
- `site/build.sh` — new, builds dist/ with version injected into footer
- `.woodpecker/site.yml` — new, CI pipeline for build/publish/deploy-staging

Depends on: none (builds on existing docker-compose generation in `bin/disinto`)

## Design

`disinto init` + `disinto up` starts two additional containers as base factory infrastructure:

### Edge proxy (Caddy)
- Reverse proxies to Forgejo and Woodpecker
- Serves staging site
- Runs on ports 80/443
- At bootstrap: IP-only, self-signed TLS or HTTP
- Domain + Let's Encrypt added later via vault resource request

### Staging container (Caddy)
- Static file server for the project's staging artifacts
- Starts with a default "Nothing shipped yet" page
- CI pipelines write to a shared volume to update staging content
- No vault approval needed — staging is the factory's sandbox

### docker-compose addition
```yaml
services:
  edge:
    image: caddy:alpine
    ports:
      - "80:80"
      - "443:443"
    volumes:
      - ./Caddyfile:/etc/caddy/Caddyfile
      - caddy_data:/data
    depends_on:
      - forgejo
      - woodpecker-server
      - staging

  staging:
    image: caddy:alpine
    volumes:
      - staging-site:/srv/site
    # Not exposed directly — edge proxies to it

volumes:
  caddy_data:
  staging-site:
```

### Caddyfile (generated by `disinto init`)
```
# IP-only at bootstrap, domain added later
:80 {
    handle /forgejo/* {
        reverse_proxy forgejo:3000
    }
    handle /ci/* {
        reverse_proxy woodpecker-server:8000
    }
    handle {
        reverse_proxy staging:80
    }
}
```

### Staging update flow
1. CI builds artifact (site tarball, etc.)
2. CI step writes to `staging-site` volume
3. Staging container serves updated content immediately
4. No restart needed — Caddy serves files directly

### Domain lifecycle
- Bootstrap: no domain, edge serves on IP
- Later: factory files vault resource request for domain
- Human buys domain, sets DNS
- Caddyfile updated with domain, Let's Encrypt auto-provisions TLS

## Affected files
- `bin/disinto` — `generate_compose()` adds edge + staging services
- New: default staging page ("Nothing shipped yet")
- New: Caddyfile template in `docker/`

## Related
- #755 — vault-gated deployment promotion (production comes later)
- #757 — ops repo (domain is a resource requested through vault)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `disinto init` generates a `docker-compose.yml` that includes `edge` (Caddy) and `staging` containers
- [ ] Edge proxy routes `/forgejo/*` → Forgejo, `/ci/*` → Woodpecker, default → staging container
- [ ] Staging container serves a default "Nothing shipped yet" page on first boot
- [ ] `docker/` directory contains a Caddyfile template generated by `disinto init`
- [ ] `disinto up` starts all containers including edge and staging without manual steps

Depends on: #747

## Design

Each agent account on the bundled Forgejo gets a `.profile` repo. This repo holds the agent's formula (copied from disinto at creation time) and its journal.

### Structure
```
{agent-bot}/.profile/
├── formula.toml        # snapshot of the formula at agent creation time
├── journal/            # daily logs of what the agent did
│   ├── 2026-03-26.md
│   └── ...
└── knowledge/          # learned patterns, best-practices (optional, agent can evolve)
```

### Lifecycle
1. **Create agent** — `disinto init` or `disinto spawn-agent` creates Forgejo account + `.profile` repo
2. **Copy formula** — current `formulas/{role}.toml` from disinto repo is copied to `.profile/formula.toml`
3. **Agent reads its own formula** — at session start, agent reads from its `.profile`, not from the disinto repo
4. **Agent writes journal** — daily entries pushed to `.profile/journal/`
5. **Agent can evolve knowledge** — best-practices, heuristics, patterns written to `.profile/knowledge/`

### What this enables

**A/B testing formulas:** Create two agents from different formula versions, run both against the same backlog, compare results (cycle time, CI pass rate, review rejection rate).

**Rollback:** New formula worse? Kill agent, spawn from older formula version.

**Audit:** What formula was this agent running when it produced that PR? Check its `.profile` at that git commit.

**Drift tracking:** Diff what an agent learned (`.profile/knowledge/`) vs what it started with. Measure formula evolution over time.

**Portability:** Move agent to different box — `git clone` its `.profile`.

### Disinto repo becomes the template

```
disinto repo:
  formulas/dev-agent.toml       ← canonical template, evolves
  formulas/review-agent.toml
  formulas/planner.toml
  ...

Running agents:
  dev-bot-v2/.profile/formula.toml   ← snapshot from formulas/dev-agent.toml@v2
  dev-bot-v3/.profile/formula.toml   ← snapshot from formulas/dev-agent.toml@v3
  review-bot/.profile/formula.toml   ← snapshot from formulas/review-agent.toml
```

The formula in the disinto repo is the template. The `.profile` copy is the instance. They can diverge — that's a feature, not a bug.

## Affected files
- `bin/disinto` — agent creation copies formula to .profile
- Agent session scripts — read formula from .profile instead of local formulas/ dir
- Planner/supervisor — can read other agents' journals from their .profile repos

## Related
- #747 — per-agent Forgejo accounts (prerequisite)
- #757 — ops repo (shared concerns stay there: vault, portfolio, resources)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `disinto spawn-agent` (or `disinto init`) creates a Forgejo account + `.profile` repo for each agent bot
- [ ] Current `formulas/{role}.toml` is copied to `.profile/formula.toml` at agent creation time
- [ ] Agent session script reads its formula from `.profile/formula.toml`, not from the repo's `formulas/` directory
- [ ] Agent writes daily journal entries to `.profile/journal/YYYY-MM-DD.md`

## Problem

`gardener/recipes/*.toml` (4 files: cascade-rebase, chicken-egg-ci, flaky-test, shellcheck-violations) are an older pattern predating `formulas/*.toml`. Two systems for the same thing.

## Fix

Migrate any unique content from recipes to the gardener formula or to new formulas. Delete the recipes directory.

## Affected files
- `gardener/recipes/*.toml` — delete after migration
- `formulas/run-gardener.toml` — absorb relevant content
- Gardener scripts that reference recipes/

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Contents of `gardener/recipes/*.toml` are diff'd against `formulas/run-gardener.toml` — any unique content is migrated
- [ ] `gardener/recipes/` directory is deleted
- [ ] No scripts in `gardener/` reference the `recipes/` path after migration
- [ ] ShellCheck passes on all modified scripts
